Tissue context: General defaults for mixed or unlisted tissue types.
UMI thresholds for general: PASS >= 2,000 | REVIEW >= 800

Top-up sequencing: RECOMMENDED
At 35.4% mean duplication, only 36.3% of the estimated library has been observed. A 1.8x top-up run on the existing library is recommended to reach ~50% duplication and ~6,005 median UMI/cell.

Cell calling

The barcode rank plot shows UMI counts from highest to lowest. The inflection separates real cells from empty droplets. A threshold of 500 UMI was applied, yielding 17,758 cells.

Cell count and UMI statistics at each threshold
UMI threshold Cells Median UMI Mean UMI
100 61,326 395.0 3,387
200 60,987 395.0 3,405
500 17,758 6,033.0 10,803
1,000 12,275 14,009.0 15,339
2,000 10,464 15,711.5 17,751
5,000 9,075 17,132.0 19,989

Duplication and PCR quality

The duplication rate (1 - unique UMIs / total reads) measures the fraction of reads that are PCR copies of molecules already counted. A narrow, symmetric distribution indicates uniform sequencing depth and unbiased PCR amplification. The optimal zone (50-75%) is marked by dotted lines.

The reads-per-UMI plot shows the molecule-level read support distribution. A geometric decay (each bar ~3x smaller than the previous) confirms no PCR jackpotting or amplification bias.

Library saturation and top-up estimate

Library size is estimated using the Michaelis-Menten model: est_library = observed_UMIs / duplication_rate. This assumes uniform sampling probability — highly expressed genes are near-complete while rare transcripts make up most of the unseen fraction.

Reads required to reach each duplication rate target
Target dup rate Total reads needed (M) Fold increase Projected median UMI/cell
50% 528 1.8 6005
60% 793 2.6 6827
70% 1,233 4.1 7567
75% 1,585 5.3 7910

Recommendation

Top-up sequencing recommended

Current duplication rate: 35.4% (below the 50% optimal threshold).
Implication: The library is under-sequenced relative to its complexity. Only 36.3% of the estimated 528,434,951 molecule library has been observed.
Library quality: The narrow duplication rate distribution and geometric reads-per-UMI decay confirm this is a sequencing depth issue — not a library preparation or PCR quality issue.
Action: Request a 1.8x top-up run on the existing library. No new library preparation is needed.

Target dup rate Total reads needed (M) Fold increase Projected median UMI/cell
50% 528 1.8x 6,005
60% 793 2.6x 6,827
70% 1,233 4.1x 7,567
75% 1,585 5.3x 7,910

Note on the saturation model: Estimates assume equal sampling probability across all molecules. In reality, highly expressed genes are already near-completely sampled; the unseen fraction (3,369 UMI/cell) is enriched for lowly expressed transcripts. Top-up sequencing disproportionately benefits detection of rare or lowly expressed genes.
